description: No example block showing invocation context or expected AI output interaction
suggested_fix: Add an example showing a Jira ticket as input and the resulting Frontend/Backend/Infrastructure plan structure as output

description: Output Requirements section describes what to include but does not show the document structure or template format
suggested_fix: Add a minimal template skeleton (e.g. headings for Requirements Summary, Technical Approach, Task Breakdown by axis) to anchor the format spec

description: No example block showing a sample branch cleanup interaction or expected terminal output
suggested_fix: Add a short example: current branch name as input, expected git output and confirmation message as output

description: No output format specified; skill does not describe what the agent should report back to the user after cleanup
suggested_fix: Add an Output section stating what confirmation message or summary should be shown after successful cleanup

description: No example block showing a full PR creation interaction with sample title and body output
suggested_fix: Add an example mirroring pr-to-production's gh pr create block, showing a feat: or fix: PR with rendered body using the appropriate template

description: PR format section specifies title constraints and language but does not show the body structure or template usage
suggested_fix: Add a minimal body format sketch or reference the three template files with a note on which sections each provides

description: Only one example provided (Group Node JSON schema); no example of a full workflow creation or editing conversation turn
suggested_fix: Add a second example showing a minimal complete workflow JSON (e.g. two subAgent nodes connected by an edge)

description: Skill does not specify what the agent should communicate to the user after applying workflow changes
suggested_fix: Add a Notes or Output section describing the expected conversational response after apply_workflow or update_nodes succeeds

description: Step 5 says 'Fix errors if any' without specifying what errors to expect, how to detect them, or what fix strategies to apply
suggested_fix: Replace with 'If apply_workflow returns an error, read the error message, correct the offending node/edge definition, and retry'

description: Identical to .github copy — same single Group Node example, no full workflow example
suggested_fix: Same as .github version; keep both in sync or use a single canonical source

description: No user-facing output format specified (identical issue to .github copy)
suggested_fix: Same fix as .github version

description: 'Fix errors if any' — same vague step as in .github copy
suggested_fix: Same fix as .github version

description: Step 4 format block uses placeholder values only; no worked example with actual file paths and verdict data
suggested_fix: Add a short concrete example row in the Details table using realistic file path, severity, and verdict values

description: Severity 'はレビューコメントのラベルまたは内容から推定する' — 'infer from content' leaves the inference criterion undefined
suggested_fix: Define severity tiers (e.g. Major: functionality broken; Minor: style/naming) and criteria for each so inference is deterministic

description: 'TypeScript 5.x (VSCode Extension Host), React 18.x (Webview UI): Follow standard conventions' — standard conventions are unspecified
suggested_fix: Link to the Biome config or add 2-3 concrete conventions (e.g. functional components only, no default exports from service files)

description: eval $(get_feature_paths) expands SPECIFY_FEATURE env var inside heredoc output; single-quote escape injection via SPECIFY_FEATURE can execute arbitrary shell commands
suggested_fix: Refactor get_feature_paths to print key=value lines and parse them with a while-read loop or declare; avoid eval entirely

description: eval $(get_feature_paths) — same SPECIFY_FEATURE injection vector as check-prerequisites.sh
suggested_fix: Same refactor: replace eval with explicit variable assignment from parsed key=value output

description: eval $(get_feature_paths) — same injection vector; in CI/CD the SPECIFY_FEATURE env var may be set by external actors via repository variables or workflow inputs
suggested_fix: Same refactor; additionally sanitize SPECIFY_FEATURE input to branch-safe characters ([a-z0-9-]) before use

description: npx -y mcp-remote installs the latest unversioned mcp-remote package at runtime with auto-accept; supply chain compromise of mcp-remote would execute in the developer environment
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific verified version: npx -y mcp-remote@x.y.z and periodically update after review

description: All dependencies use caret ranges (^); silent minor-version upgrades can introduce vulnerabilities or breaking changes
suggested_fix: Lock security-sensitive packages (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk, @slack/web-api) to exact versions; use npm ci in CI pipelines

description: cc-workflow-ai-editor SKILL.md is duplicated verbatim in .github/skills/ and .roo/skills/; divergence risk if one copy is updated without the other
suggested_fix: Maintain one canonical source and symlink or copy-on-build to both destinations; add a CI check that verifies the two files are byte-identical
